skill.name: pipedrive-automation
skill.description: Automate Pipedrive CRM operations including deals, contacts, organizations, activities, notes, and pipeline management via Rube MCP (Composio).
skill.summary: Comprehensive documentation for automating Pipedrive CRM workflows through Rube MCP server integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only (markdown file) with no executable code. Static scanner produced 219 false positives by misinterpreting markdown backticks (code formatting) as Ruby shell execution. The only real risk is network access to the Rube MCP endpoint (https://rube.app/mcp), which is legitimate for MCP server connectivity. No code execution, file system, or environment variable risks exist.

low_findings.title: External MCP Server Connection
low_findings.description: Skill requires connection to external MCP server at https://rube.app/mcp for Pipedrive API operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear documentation of external service dependency - users must trust the Rube MCP provider for credential handling.

content.user_title: Automate Pipedrive CRM Operations with AI
content.value_statement: Eliminate manual CRM data entry and streamline sales workflows. This skill enables AI assistants to manage deals, contacts, activities, and pipelines directly through Pipedrive's API via Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Create, update, and search deals in Pipedrive pipelines with org/contact linking
Manage contacts (persons and organizations) including creation, updates, and lookups
Schedule and track activities like calls, meetings, and tasks linked to deals or contacts
Add and manage notes attached to deals, persons, organizations, or projects
Query pipelines and stages to retrieve deal counts and pipeline summaries
Resolve entity names to IDs and handle pagination for large datasets
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP server connection and Pipedrive OAuth setup
Cannot execute custom Pipedrive API calls outside documented tool catalog
Rate limited by Pipedrive's per-company API limits for bulk operations
Lead operations use UUID format while other entities use numeric IDs

use_cases.title: Sales Operations Automation
use_cases.description: Automatically create deals from incoming leads, link them to existing organizations, and schedule follow-up activities based on deal stage changes.
use_cases.target_user: Sales operations managers handling high-volume deal pipelines

use_cases.title: CRM Data Synchronization
use_cases.description: Keep contact and organization records up to date by searching for duplicates, merging records, and updating contact details from external sources.
use_cases.target_user: Revenue operations teams maintaining CRM data quality

use_cases.title: Activity Tracking and Reporting
use_cases.description: Log calls, meetings, and tasks automatically from calendar events or communication logs, then generate activity reports by user or pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Deal
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new deal in Pipedrive for [Company Name] with value [amount] [currency]. Link it to contact [contact name] and place it in the [pipeline name] pipeline at stage [stage name]. Set expected close date to [date].

prompt_templates.title: Search and Update Contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for a contact by email [email address] in Pipedrive. If found, update their phone number to [phone] and add a note about [topic]. If not found, create a new contact record.

prompt_templates.title: Schedule Follow-up Activity
prompt_templates.prompt: For deal [deal name], schedule a [call/meeting/task] for [date] at [time] with duration [minutes] minutes. Set the subject to [subject] and link it to the primary contact on the deal.

prompt_templates.title: Pipeline Health Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Get all pipelines in Pipedrive and for each pipeline, retrieve all stages with deal counts. Summarize the total deal value per stage and identify any deals that have been in the same stage for more than 30 days.

output_examples.input: Create a deal for Acme Corp worth $50,000 USD in the Sales Pipeline at Proposal stage
output_examples.output: Deal created successfully: 'Acme Corp - Q1 2026' (ID: 12847) with value $50,000 USD placed in Proposal stage of Sales Pipeline. Linked to organization Acme Corp (ID: 3921).

output_examples.input: Find all contacts with email containing @acme.com
output_examples.output: Found 3 contacts: John Smith (john@acme.com), Jane Doe (jane@acme.com), Bob Wilson (bob@acme.com). All linked to Acme Corp organization.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Search for existing contacts and organizations before creating new ones to prevent duplicates
Use pagination parameters (start, limit) when listing persons or organizations to handle large datasets
Check response.additional_data.didMerge after organization creation to detect auto-merges
Resolve display names to numeric IDs using search endpoints before create/update operations
anti_patterns: Creating contacts or organizations without first searching for existing records by name or email
Assuming boolean values for 'done' field on activities - it must be integer 0 or 1
Passing email or phone as plain strings instead of arrays of objects with value, label, and primary fields
Ignoring pagination indicators and missing records when processing large contact lists
Using wildcard characters in search terms - Pipedrive search requires minimum 2 characters without wildcards

faq.question: How do I connect Pipedrive to this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'pipedrive' and follow the OAuth link if connection is not active.

faq.question: What is the difference between persons and organizations?
faq.answer: Persons are individual contacts (with email, phone arrays). Organizations are companies or businesses. Deals and activities can be linked to either or both.

faq.question: Why do custom fields show as long hash keys in responses?
faq.answer: Pipedrive stores custom fields with alphanumeric hash identifiers. Use the respective Fields endpoints (dealFields, personFields, etc.) to map these hashes to readable names.

faq.question: Can I delete contacts or organizations permanently?
faq.answer: Deletion is soft-delete with 30-day retention. Items are permanently deleted after 30 days. Use PIPEDRIVE_DELETE_A_PERSON or PIPEDRIVE_DELETE_AN_ORGANIZATION to initiate deletion.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large contact lists?
faq.answer: Use 'start' (offset) and 'limit' (page size) parameters. Check additional_data.pagination.more_items_in_collection to know if more pages exist, and use next_start for the next page offset.

faq.question: What activity types are available?
faq.answer: Activity types depend on your Pipedrive account configuration. Common types include 'call', 'meeting', 'task', and 'email'. The type must match an existing ActivityTypes key_string in your account.
